requirements:
Requirement 1: The system shall provide secure user authentication for all roles (Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, Administrative Staff).

Requirement 2: The system shall support role-based access control that enforces permissions for Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, and Administrative Staff.

Requirement 3: The Administrator shall be able to create, edit, deactivate, reactivate, and delete user accounts for all roles.

Requirement 4: The system shall require unique identifiers for each user (e.g., student ID, teacher ID) and shall prevent duplicate user accounts.

Requirement 5: The system shall allow Administrators to import and export users in bulk via common formats (CSV, XLSX) with validation and error reporting.

Requirement 6: The system shall allow Administrators to assign one or more roles to a user and to change user roles.

Requirement 7: The system shall allow Administrators to reset user passwords and to configure password policies (minimum length, complexity, expiration).

Requirement 8: The system shall support password recovery (password reset via secure email link) and optional multi-factor authentication for all users.

Requirement 9: The system shall lock accounts after a configurable number of failed login attempts and shall provide an unlock mechanism for Administrators.

Requirement 10: The Administrator shall be able to create, edit, and delete teaching modules, including module name, code, description, schedule, and capacity.

Requirement 11: The system shall allow a teaching module to be associated with one or more Referring Teachers.

Requirement 12: The Administrator shall be able to assign and reassign Referring Teachers to modules and to view current associations.

Requirement 13: The Administrator shall be able to associate Teachers and Students with modules and to manage enrollments.

Requirement 14: The system shall enforce referential integrity so that modules cannot reference non-existent users.

Requirement 15: The global user database shall store and manage Teacher and Student profiles with contact details, enrollment history, and role history.

Requirement 16: The Referring Teacher shall be able to add Teachers and Students to a module only if those Teachers and Students already exist in the global database.

Requirement 17: The system shall provide a search interface for Referring Teachers to find existing Teachers and Students in the global database before adding them.

Requirement 18: If a Referring Teacher attempts to add a Teacher or Student not present in the database, the system shall provide a clear error and an option to request that the Administrator create the user account.

Requirement 19: The system shall allow Referring Teachers to request bulk additions of module enrollments that Administrators can approve or reject.

Requirement 20: The system shall record and display the full list of Teachers and Students grouped by module.

Requirement 21: The system shall allow Teachers to report absences for students enrolled in their modules and for themselves in their scheduled sessions.

Requirement 22: When a Teacher reports an absence in a module, the system shall allow the Teacher to add a free-text comment limited to a configurable length.

Requirement 23: The system shall validate that an absence reported by a Teacher pertains to a scheduled session of the specified module and to students enrolled in that module.

Requirement 24: The system shall prevent duplicate absences for the same student, same module, same session (date/time) by the same or different Teachers, and shall provide options to merge or mark duplicates.

Requirement 25: Teachers shall be able to mark the type of absence (single session absence, partial session, late arrival, early departure) and duration (start time, end time).

Requirement 26: The system shall allow Teachers to mark multiple students absent in a single operation (bulk absence marking).

Requirement 27: Teachers shall be able to view and filter the list of absences for all students enrolled in any module they teach, with filters by date range, status, module, student, and teacher.

Requirement 28: The system shall present absence records including date/time, module, reporting Teacher, comment, status (unjustified/justified/pending), and any attached justification documents.

Requirement 29: Administrative Staff shall have access to all student absences across the institution, with filters by module, student, date range, status, and Referring Teacher.

Requirement 30: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to enter the reason for an absence and to set the justification status (justified, unjustified, pending).

Requirement 31: Administrative Staff shall be able to attach documents (e.g., medical certificates) to an absence record and the system shall validate file types and sizes.

Requirement 32: The system shall maintain an auditable history of all changes to absence records including who made the change and timestamps.

Requirement 33: Absences shall be groupable and viewable by module and by student, with aggregated counts and details.

Requirement 34: The system shall provide a dashboard for Teachers showing recent absences in their modules and alerts for students who exceed configured thresholds.

Requirement 35: The system shall provide a dashboard for Referring Teachers showing module enrollments, recent absences, and pending actions.

Requirement 36: The system shall provide a dashboard for Administrative Staff showing institution-wide absence statistics, pending justifications, and alerts for policy violations.

Requirement 37: The system shall send notifications (email and/or in-app) to Students when an absence is recorded for them, including details and any Teacher comments.

Requirement 38: The system shall send notifications to Teachers and Referring Teachers when Administrative Staff changes the justification status of an absence.

Requirement 39: The system shall send notifications to Administrative Staff when Teachers report absences that require administrative review.

Requirement 40: The system shall send alerts to teaching staff (Teachers and Referring Teachers) listing students who have more than three unjustified absences, and this threshold shall be configurable by Administrators.

Requirement 41: Alerts for students exceeding unjustified absence thresholds shall be available on a dedicated page and optionally sent via email at configurable intervals (real-time, daily, weekly).

Requirement 42: The system shall allow per-student tracking of the number of justified and unjustified absences and shall make the counts available to authorized roles.

Requirement 43: Students shall be able to view their own absence records, including dates, modules, reporting Teacher, comments, and justification status.

Requirement 44: The system shall allow Students to submit justifications for their recorded absences, including free-text reason and optional attachments, within a configurable time window.

Requirement 45: The system shall track submission timestamps for student justifications and enforce the justification deadline configured by Administrators.

Requirement 46: Administrative Staff shall be able to approve, reject, or request more information for student-submitted justifications, with automated notifications of the decision to the Student and relevant Teachers.

Requirement 47: The system shall maintain a workflow status for each absence (reported, justification_submitted, admin_review, justified, unjustified, appealed) and permit transitions defined by Administrators.

Requirement 48: The system shall allow Students to appeal unjustified decisions with supplementary documents; Administrative Staff shall be able to process and record appeal outcomes.

Requirement 49: The system shall provide role-appropriate views so Teachers can see which absences are justified/unjustified and any administrative notes.

Requirement 50: The system shall provide the ability to export absence lists and aggregated reports by module, student, teacher, date range, and status in CSV and PDF formats.

Requirement 51: The system shall provide printable views for modules, attendance sheets, and absence reports.

Requirement 52: The system shall allow Administrators to configure absence policies, including thresholds for alerts, justification deadlines, allowed attachment types, and retention periods.

Requirement 53: The system shall enforce data validation on all input fields (dates, times, IDs, email addresses) and display clear validation messages.

Requirement 54: The system shall record the date and time zone of each absence entry and display dates/times in the user’s preferred time zone.

Requirement 55: The system shall allow Administrators to deactivate modules and define behavior for existing absence and enrollment records (archive, cascade delete, or prevent deletion).

Requirement 56: The system shall prevent deletion of users or modules if associated records (e.g., absences) would be orphaned, unless an Administrator explicitly confirms archival or deletion and records that action in an audit log.

Requirement 57: The system shall provide an audit log of all administrative actions (user creation, role assignment, module creation, deletion, justification decisions) that is searchable and exportable.

Requirement 58: The system shall provide fine-grained search and filtering for users, modules, and absences with support for pagination and sorting.

Requirement 59: The system shall handle concurrent edits safely, providing optimistic locking or edit conflict resolution when multiple users attempt to modify the same absence, enrollment, or module.

Requirement 60: The system shall debounce bulk operations and provide progress and success/failure reporting for long-running imports or exports.

Requirement 61: The system shall provide API endpoints (secure, authenticated) for integration with external systems (student information system, calendar, email) and support rate limiting and API key management.

Requirement 62: The system shall integrate with calendar systems (e.g., iCal, Google Calendar) to import module schedules and to allow export of relevant sessions.

Requirement 63: The system shall validate consistency between module schedules and reported absences (e.g., prevent reporting for non-existent sessions unless explicitly allowed with justification).

Requirement 64: The system shall provide role-based configurable automated reminders to students to submit justifications before expiration of the allowable period.

Requirement 65: The system shall provide a configurable threshold for late-justifications and mark them as late in the record.

Requirement 66: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to add administrative-only comments on absence records that are visible to authorized staff but not necessarily to students.

Requirement 67: The system shall provide localization support and allow content and UI labels to be translated; the default language shall be configurable by Administrators.

Requirement 68: The system shall be responsive and usable on desktop, tablet, and mobile devices and support modern browsers.

Requirement 69: The system shall comply with applicable data protection and privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR) including consent management, data access requests, and right to be forgotten workflows for personal data.

Requirement 70: The system shall encrypt sensitive data at rest and in transit using industry-standard algorithms (e.g., TLS for transport, AES for storage).

Requirement 71: The system shall enforce that only authorized roles can view personally identifiable information and sensitive documents, using role-based permissions and access controls.

Requirement 72: The system shall provide secure file storage for attachments with virus/malware scanning and configurable maximum file sizes.

Requirement 73: The system shall deliver email notifications reliably with retry logic and provide status logs for notification delivery and failures.

Requirement 74: The system shall guarantee response times of under 2 seconds for standard UI queries under expected load and shall degrade gracefully under heavy load.

Requirement 75: The system shall be able to handle concurrent usage from a configurable number of users (e.g., initial target 5,000 concurrent sessions) and be scalable horizontally.

Requirement 76: The system shall provide at least 99.5% availability (uptime) excluding scheduled maintenance windows.

Requirement 77: The system shall provide daily backups of databases and stored documents and allow restoration to a point-in-time within a configurable retention period.

Requirement 78: The system shall provide monitoring, alerting, and logs for system health, errors, performance metrics, and security incidents.

Requirement 79: The system shall implement input sanitization and protection against common web vulnerabilities (e.g., XSS, CSRF, SQL injection).

Requirement 80: The system shall require secure session management with configurable session timeout and protection against session fixation.

Requirement 81: The system shall allow Administrators to configure retention and archival policies for absence records (e.g., archive records older than X years) and to purge data per legal requirements.

Requirement 82: The system shall enforce business rules such as preventing Teachers from reporting absences for students not enrolled, preventing Students from submitting justifications after deadline unless a late-justification workflow is triggered, and preventing duplication.

Requirement 83: The system shall provide comprehensive role-based help and documentation accessible in-app (user guides for Administrators, Teachers, Referring Teachers, Administrative Staff, and Students).

Requirement 84: The system shall provide real-time indicators and confirmations for user actions (e.g., absence saved, justification submitted) and shall present meaningful error messages.

Requirement 85: The system shall provide PDF/CSV export templates for compliance and audit use and shall include metadata (exported by, timestamp, filters applied).

Requirement 86: The system shall enable Administrators to configure email templates and in-app notification messages with variables for personalization.

Requirement 87: The system shall provide a configurable alerting page that can be filtered by module, Referring Teacher, or student group and that highlights students above the unjustified-absence threshold.

Requirement 88: The system shall provide metrics and charts for absence trends over time (by module, student cohort, Teacher) and enable exporting these analytics.

Requirement 89: The system shall ensure time zone consistency for multi-campus or remote users and shall provide options to view times in local or campus time.

Requirement 90: The system shall provide a secure method for external Referring Teachers or staff who are not regular users to request user creation or report attendance exceptions, with audit trails.

Requirement 91: The system shall provide data validation and business-rule checking on imports and shall present a report of import errors and warnings for Administrator review.

Requirement 92: The system shall ensure actions that affect many records (bulk delete, bulk justify/unjustify) require confirmation and shall be reversible via undo within a configurable window or via an administrative audit-based restore.

Requirement 93: The system shall keep versioned history of critical records (absence entries, justification decisions, module assignments) and allow Administrators to view prior versions.

Requirement 94: The system shall support configurable business hours and holiday calendars to suppress false absence alerts for scheduled non-instructional days.

Requirement 95: The system shall allow mapping of Teachers to substitute Teachers and shall support reporting absences by substitute Teachers with clear attribution.

Requirement 96: The system shall provide an API or UI action for Teachers to mark an entire session as canceled (e.g., class canceled) and to convert pending absences accordingly with notifications.

Requirement 97: The system shall enforce data ownership and exportability so that Students and Trainers can obtain a copy of their records upon request.

Requirement 98: The system shall perform input length limits, character encoding handling, and reject invalid characters in names and free-text fields to prevent data corruption.

Requirement 99: The system shall provide role-specific reports exportable on demand and scheduled periodic reports (daily/weekly/monthly) deliverable via email or available in the UI.

Requirement 100: The system shall support localization of date and time formats, number formats, and currency where used.

Requirement 101: The system shall ensure accessibility conformance to WCAG 2.1 AA guidelines for users with disabilities.

Requirement 102: The system shall provide clear workflows and data protections for sensitive situations (e.g., minors) including parental contact permissions and guardianship data fields managed by Administrators.

Requirement 103: The system shall provide configurable retention of notification history for compliance and auditing.

Requirement 104: The system shall provide an administrator-configurable policy for what constitutes an "unjustified" absence and how corrective actions or disciplinary workflows are triggered.

Requirement 105: The system shall allow linking of absence records to disciplinary records or student support cases, with access limited to authorized Administrative Staff.

Requirement 106: The system shall log and alert on suspicious activity (e.g., mass deletion, frequent failed logins) and provide incident response workflows.

Requirement 107: The system shall provide an exportable list of students exceeding unjustified absence thresholds, including module context and dates.

Requirement 108: The system shall provide the ability to tag or categorize absences (e.g., medical, personal, excused) and allow Administrators to manage tag lists.

Requirement 109: The system shall include client-side and server-side validation to ensure consistent enforcement of rules and to prevent circumvention.

Requirement 110: The system shall include automated tests and unit/integration test coverage for core workflows (user management, module management, absence recording, justification workflow) as part of delivery.

Requirement 111: The system shall provide administrative impersonation capability (audit logged) so Administrators can view the system exactly as a given user to troubleshoot, with strict controls and logging.

Requirement 112: The system shall maintain an SLA for support response times for critical incidents and shall provide a contact channel for system support.

Requirement 113: The system shall provide mechanisms to archive historical modules and cohorts while maintaining linked absence records accessible in read-only mode.

Requirement 114: The system shall allow configuration of who receives which types of notifications (global and per-module overrides), and allow users to set personal notification preferences where appropriate.

Requirement 115: The system shall ensure that deletion or anonymization of a student for privacy reasons preserves aggregated statistical data unless legally required to purge.

Requirement 116: The system shall provide a sandbox or staging environment that mirrors production for testing imports, bulk operations, and policy configuration before applying changes to live data.

Requirement 117: The system shall provide consistent timezone-aware scheduling for sessions and allow Administrators to correct misreported session associations if teachers misattribute an absence to a wrong session.

Requirement 118: The system shall include rate limiting and monitoring on public endpoints (e.g., login, API) to mitigate abuse and DDoS threats.

Requirement 119: The system shall implement a clear error reporting mechanism to capture uncaught exceptions and provide user-friendly error pages with reference codes for support.

Requirement 120: The system shall provide a migration and data conversion plan and tools for onboarding historical attendance records into the system with provenance preserved.

Requirement 1: The system shall provide secure user accounts for five roles: Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, and Administrative Staff.

Requirement 2: The system shall require authentication for all users before accessing any protected functionality.

Requirement 3: The system shall support role-based access control that enforces permissions for Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, and Administrative Staff.

Requirement 4: The system shall allow Administrators to create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) teaching modules.

Requirement 5: The system shall allow Administrators to associate one or more Referring Teachers with each teaching module.

Requirement 6: The system shall allow Administrators to assign multiple Teachers to a teaching module.

Requirement 7: The system shall allow Administrators to add, edit, and remove users (students, teachers, referring teachers, administrative staff) in the global user database.

Requirement 8: The system shall allow Administrators to manage user attributes including name, email, student/employee ID, role, contact details, enrollment status, and metadata.

Requirement 9: The system shall allow Administrators to enable, disable, or soft-delete user accounts while preserving historical attendance records.

Requirement 10: The system shall enforce unique identifiers (e.g., student ID or email) to prevent duplicate user accounts.

Requirement 11: The system shall allow Referring Teachers to add existing registered Teachers to modules, with a search or lookup of the global user database.

Requirement 12: The system shall allow Referring Teachers to add existing registered Students to modules, with a search or lookup of the global user database.

Requirement 13: The system shall prevent Referring Teachers from creating new user accounts; they may only link existing registered users to modules.

Requirement 14: The system shall allow Referring Teachers to view and manage the roster (list of students and teachers) of modules they refer.

Requirement 15: The system shall allow Teachers to record an absence event for a student in a module they teach.

Requirement 16: The system shall allow Teachers to record an absence event for themselves in a module they teach.

Requirement 17: The system shall record the date and time (timestamp) and the session or class occurrence when an absence is recorded.

Requirement 18: The system shall allow Teachers to add an optional textual comment when reporting an absence.

Requirement 19: The system shall allow Teachers to indicate the type of absence (e.g., full session absence, partial absence, late) when recording an absence.

Requirement 20: The system shall enforce validation to prevent recording an absence outside the scheduled session times unless explicitly allowed with justification.

Requirement 21: The system shall prevent duplicate absence records for the same student, module, and session unless an override is explicitly performed with an audit record.

Requirement 22: The system shall provide Teachers with a view that lists all absences for students enrolled in modules the Teacher is assigned to.

Requirement 23: The system shall allow Teachers to filter the absence list by module, student, date range, absence type, and justification status.

Requirement 24: The system shall allow Teachers to sort and paginate the absence list.

Requirement 25: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to access and view all student absences across all modules.

Requirement 26: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to enter or edit the official reason for an absence and mark an absence as justified or unjustified.

Requirement 27: The system shall maintain a status field for each absence with states including: Recorded, Pending Justification, Justified, Unjustified, Reviewed, and Archived.

Requirement 28: The system shall allow Students to view their own absence records with associated comments and status.

Requirement 29: The system shall allow Students to submit justifications for their absences, including textual explanation and file attachments (e.g., medical certificates).

Requirement 30: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to view submitted justification documents and accept, reject, or request more information.

Requirement 31: The system shall allow Teachers and Referring Teachers to view submitted student justifications and the resulting administrative decision.

Requirement 32: The system shall support upload of justification documents with file type and size restrictions enforced (configurable allowed types and maximum size).

Requirement 33: The system shall scan uploaded files for viruses and malware before storing.

Requirement 34: The system shall keep an immutable audit trail for all changes to absence records, including who made the change, timestamp, and previous values.

Requirement 35: The system shall allow authorized users to edit absence records only where permitted by role and only with the change recorded in the audit trail.

Requirement 36: The system shall allow authorized users to delete absence records only as soft-deletes with preserved history and audit markers.

Requirement 37: The system shall provide an alerts page that lists students with more than three (>) unjustified absences.

Requirement 38: The system shall allow configuration of the threshold that triggers the “excessive unjustified absences” alert.

Requirement 39: The system shall provide the alerts page to Referring Teachers and Teachers assigned to the affected students and to Administrators and Administrative Staff.

Requirement 40: The system shall allow filtering and sorting within the alerts page by module, class, student, number of unjustified absences, and date range.

Requirement 41: The system shall allow exporting alerts and absence lists to CSV and PDF formats.

Requirement 42: The system shall group absence records by module and by student in views and reports, allowing the user to toggle grouping.

Requirement 43: The system shall provide aggregated reports showing absence counts per student, per module, per teacher, and per time period.

Requirement 44: The system shall provide trend charts and summary statistics for absences over time (configurable date ranges).

Requirement 45: The system shall support bulk operations including bulk import of users, bulk assignment of students to modules, bulk marking of absences, and bulk justification processing.

Requirement 46: The system shall validate bulk imports for format errors, duplicates, referential integrity, and provide a preview and error report before applying.

Requirement 47: The system shall prevent assignment of a user to a module if the user does not exist in the global database, and shall provide an explicit error message.

Requirement 48: The system shall allow Administrators to define module schedules (days, times, academic periods) and link absences to scheduled sessions.

Requirement 49: The system shall allow Teachers to record absences retrospectively with an indication that the absence was recorded late, and the system shall record the recording timestamp and reason for late recording.

Requirement 50: The system shall implement business rules to determine whether a justification submitted after a configurable time window is acceptable.

Requirement 51: The system shall notify Students when an absence has been recorded for them.

Requirement 52: The system shall notify Teachers and Referring Teachers when a student in their module reaches the alert threshold of unjustified absences.

Requirement 53: The system shall notify Administrative Staff when a justification is submitted by a student.

Requirement 54: The system shall support configurable notification channels including in-app notifications and email, and optionally SMS.

Requirement 55: The system shall allow users to opt in/out of email or SMS notifications where permitted by policy.

Requirement 56: The system shall provide real-time or near-real-time notifications for critical events (absence recorded, justification submitted, threshold reached).

Requirement 57: The system shall provide a dashboard for each role summarizing relevant data: Administrators (system stats, unresolved justifications), Referring Teachers (module rosters, alerts), Teachers (today’s sessions and absences), Students (their absence history and pending actions), Administrative Staff (pending justifications and unresolved cases).

Requirement 58: The system shall provide search functionality to find students, teachers, modules, and absence records by ID, name, module code, and other attributes.

Requirement 59: The system shall support pagination, sorting, and server-side filtering for large result sets.

Requirement 60: The system shall provide an activity log for Administrative Staff and Administrators showing recent system actions, errors, and key events.

Requirement 61: The system shall provide APIs (RESTful or GraphQL) for integration with external Student Information Systems, authentication providers, and reporting tools.

Requirement 62: The system shall support single sign-on (SSO) via SAML 2.0 and OAuth2/OIDC integrations.

Requirement 63: The system shall require strong password policies including minimum length, complexity, expiration policy (configurable), and account lockout after configurable failed attempts.

Requirement 64: The system shall support two-factor authentication (2FA) for Administrator and Administrative Staff accounts.

Requirement 65: The system shall encrypt all data in transit using TLS 1.2+ and sensitive personal data at rest using strong encryption.

Requirement 66: The system shall restrict access to justification documents and personal data to authorized roles only.

Requirement 67: The system shall support data export and subject data requests for compliance (e.g., download all personal data for a student).

Requirement 68: The system shall support data deletion workflows to comply with privacy regulations, including soft-delete and secure purge after retention period.

Requirement 69: The system shall allow configuration of data retention periods for attendance records, justifications, and files.

Requirement 70: The system shall log all authentication attempts, successful logins, failed logins, and account changes with timestamps and source IPs.

Requirement 71: The system shall provide protection against common web vulnerabilities (CSRF, XSS, SQL injection) and validate all inputs server-side and client-side.

Requirement 72: The system shall implement rate limiting for critical endpoints to prevent abuse and denial-of-service.

Requirement 73: The system shall implement role-based UI elements so users only see features they are authorized to access.

Requirement 74: The system shall ensure that Teachers can only view absence records and students for modules to which they are assigned.

Requirement 75: The system shall ensure that Referring Teachers can only add/link users to modules they refer and can only view rosters and absences for modules they refer.

Requirement 76: The system shall ensure that Students can only see their own records and not other students’ personal information.

Requirement 77: The system shall provide internationalization (i18n) support and configurable language options for the user interface.

Requirement 78: The system shall be responsive and usable on desktop, tablet, and mobile devices.

Requirement 79: The system shall meet WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility standards for critical screens and workflows.

Requirement 80: The system shall provide clear user feedback for success, validation errors, and system errors with actionable guidance.

Requirement 81: The system shall provide contextual help, tooltips, and a help center or documentation for common tasks.

Requirement 82: The system shall provide undo/confirm dialogs for destructive actions (e.g., deleting modules, removing students from modules).

Requirement 83: The system shall support transactional integrity so that multi-step operations (e.g., bulk assignments) are atomic or present a clear partial failure state with rollback options.

Requirement 84: The system shall support scalable architecture capable of handling concurrent users in the expected deployment size (configurable target e.g., thousands of users) and allow horizontal scaling.

Requirement 85: The system shall provide average page response times under normal load of less than 2 seconds for primary user flows.

Requirement 86: The system shall support scheduled backups of databases and file storage with a recoverability target (configurable RTO/RPO, e.g., RTO 4 hours, RPO 1 hour).

Requirement 87: The system shall provide monitoring and alerting for system health metrics (CPU, memory, disk, error rates), and send alerts to administrators on critical failures.

Requirement 88: The system shall have an uptime objective appropriate for educational operations (e.g., 99.9% monthly availability) and planned maintenance windows.

Requirement 89: The system shall provide import/export audit reports for all bulk operations showing successes, failures, and row-level error messages.

Requirement 90: The system shall validate all date/time inputs against the system calendar and the module schedule timezone and prevent inconsistent timezone-related errors.

Requirement 91: The system shall support configurable academic periods/semesters and associate modules and absences with a specific academic period.

Requirement 92: The system shall allow Administrators to configure absence types, statuses, and business rules without code changes.

Requirement 93: The system shall implement input validation rules for textual comments (length limits, prohibited content filtering) and provide character limits.

Requirement 94: The system shall enforce file attachment quotas per user and per module to prevent excessive storage consumption.

Requirement 95: The system shall provide secure storage for uploaded documents with per-file access controls and the ability to revoke access.

Requirement 96: The system shall ensure referential integrity so that deletion of modules, users, or roles is blocked or cascaded according to configured policies and accompanied by notifications and audit records.

Requirement 97: The system shall preserve historical attendance and justification records even if a user changes role or is unassigned from a module.

Requirement 98: The system shall provide role-specific audit reports listing who performed approvals, justifications, edits, and deletions for compliance reviews.

Requirement 99: The system shall allow Administrators to restore soft-deleted users, modules, and absence records within a configurable restoration window.

Requirement 100: The system shall provide exportable weekly and monthly reports for Administrative Staff showing unresolved justifications and students approaching disciplinary thresholds.

Requirement 101: The system shall allow Teachers and Referring Teachers to add internal notes to student records visible only to authorized staff.

Requirement 102: The system shall prevent Teachers from modifying administrative decisions (e.g., re-justifying an absence marked unjustified by Administrative Staff) unless Administrator privileges are used, with changes audited.

Requirement 103: The system shall support timezone-aware scheduling so users in different timezones see consistent session times.

Requirement 104: The system shall provide confirmation receipts to Students and Teachers when justification documents are submitted or processed.

Requirement 105: The system shall include role-based dashboards showing counts of pending actions (pending justifications, unresolved alerts) and links to relevant lists.

Requirement 106: The system shall support scheduling automated reports and alerts to be delivered to configured email lists or groups on a schedule.

Requirement 107: The system shall provide a secure administrator console for system configuration, user management, threshold settings, and audit access.

Requirement 108: The system shall require multi-tenancy controls if used by multiple institutions, ensuring strict data segregation per tenant.

Requirement 109: The system shall provide a test/staging environment that mirrors production for safe testing of updates and bulk operations.

Requirement 110: The system shall be implemented with automated unit tests, integration tests, and acceptance tests covering core workflows for continuous integration.

Requirement 111: The system shall undergo regular security assessments and penetration testing, with remediation tracking.

Requirement 112: The system shall provide versioning for exported reports and be able to reproduce historical views of alerts and absence counts for audit dates.

Requirement 113: The system shall enforce minimum and maximum limits on the number of modules a teacher can be assigned to (configurable) and validate assignments.

Requirement 114: The system shall provide a configurable grace period for justifications submitted after an absence before the absence is automatically marked unjustified.

Requirement 115: The system shall provide conflict detection and resolution when two users attempt to update the same absence record concurrently.

Requirement 116: The system shall display the current status of the module rosters and last updated timestamps for transparency.

Requirement 117: The system shall maintain a changelog of module associations when modules have their referring teachers updated, including previous associations.

Requirement 118: The system shall notify affected Teachers and Referring Teachers when an Administrator modifies module-teacher associations.

Requirement 119: The system shall provide a mechanism for students to report errors in their attendance records and for staff to track and resolve these disputes.

Requirement 120: The system shall enforce legal and policy constraints for minors, such as parental notification and consent workflows, where applicable and configurable.

Requirement 121: The system shall provide anonymized aggregate data for reporting and research without exposing personally identifiable information.

Requirement 122: The system shall implement graceful error handling with logging and user-friendly messages and provide an error reporting mechanism to administrators.

Requirement 123: The system shall provide time-limited access tokens for API access and support revocation of tokens.

Requirement 124: The system shall provide capability to archive historical modules and associated attendance data and to restore them when needed.

Requirement 125: The system shall allow configurable business rules to automatically escalate students to administrative review when threshold conditions are met (e.g., >3 unjustified absences).

Requirement 126: The system shall maintain a mapping of modules to academic courses and classes for hierarchical reporting.

Requirement 127: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to bulk-update absence statuses (e.g., mark multiple absences as justified) with audit trails and validation.

Requirement 128: The system shall allow Administrators to export the entire global database (users, modules, enrollments, absences) in a machine-readable format for archival or migration.

Requirement 129: The system shall provide scheduled maintenance notifications and display maintenance windows in the UI for end-users.

Requirement 130: The system shall support localization of date and time formats and number formats according to user preferences.

Requirement 131: The system shall provide clear escalation paths and contact information for support within the application.

Requirement 132: The system shall support configurable retention and deletion policies for audit logs and monitoring data to balance compliance and storage costs.

Requirement 133: The system shall allow Admins to configure and enforce a policy for maximum allowed unjustified absences before automated actions (notifications, escalations, flags) are taken.

Requirement 134: The system shall provide filtering to identify students with patterns of partial absences or tardiness and generate alerts accordingly.

Requirement 135: The system shall ensure all date and time-based calculations (e.g., counting absences toward threshold) are auditable and reproducible.

Requirement 136: The system shall provide metrics on system usage (logins, daily active users, absences recorded per day) for administrators.

Requirement 137: The system shall support a consent mechanism for collecting and storing personal data where required by law and record consent events.

Requirement 138: The system shall provide version-controlled release notes and change logs for all functional changes visible to administrators.

Requirement 139: The system shall include an onboarding workflow for new Administrators and Referring Teachers to learn how to add/link users and configure modules.

Requirement 140: The system shall offer configurable retention of justification attachments and the ability for Administrators to purge attachments manually when required.

Requirement 1: The system shall provide role-based authentication for Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, and Administrative Staff accounts.

Requirement 2: The system shall support secure user registration with email verification and administrator approval workflows.

Requirement 3: The system shall allow Administrators to create, read, update, and delete teaching modules.

Requirement 4: The system shall allow Administrators to associate one or more Referring Teachers to each teaching module.

Requirement 5: The system shall allow Administrators to assign Teachers and Referring Teachers to modules and to remove those assignments.

Requirement 6: The system shall maintain a global database of all Teachers and Students that Administrators can create, read, update, and delete.

Requirement 7: The system shall allow Administrators to import and export Teacher and Student records in common formats (CSV and XLSX).

Requirement 8: The system shall require unique identifiers (e.g., student ID) for all student and teacher records and validate uniqueness on create and import.

Requirement 9: The system shall allow Referring Teachers to add Teachers and Students to modules only if those user records already exist in the global database.

Requirement 10: The system shall allow Referring Teachers to request creation of new Teacher or Student records when a requested record does not exist, subject to Administrator approval.

Requirement 11: The system shall allow Teachers assigned to a module to record an absence for a student for a specific module session.

Requirement 12: The system shall allow Teachers to record absence type categories including absent, late, left early, partial absence, and other custom types.

Requirement 13: The system shall require Teachers to enter the date and time (or session identifier) when recording an absence and validate that the timestamp is not inconsistent with the module schedule.

Requirement 14: The system shall allow Teachers to add an optional free-text comment when recording an absence.

Requirement 15: The system shall prevent duplicate absence records for the same student, module, and session and shall flag potential duplicates for review.

Requirement 16: The system shall allow Teachers to view a list of all absences for students enrolled in any module to which they are assigned.

Requirement 17: The system shall allow Teachers to filter and search the absence list by student name, student ID, module, date range, absence type, and justification status.

Requirement 18: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to view and search all student absences across all modules without restriction.

Requirement 19: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to enter a reason for an absence and associate supporting documentation (file upload) to the absence record.

Requirement 20: The system shall allow Students to submit a justification request for an absence, including a textual explanation and file attachments (e.g., medical certificate).

Requirement 21: The system shall track the justification lifecycle states for each absence (e.g., pending, justified, rejected, waived) and record the user and timestamp for each state change.

Requirement 22: The system shall allow Administrative Staff and Administrators to accept, reject, or modify student justifications and to add administrative reasons and comments.

Requirement 23: The system shall automatically mark an absence as unjustified by default until an Administrative Staff or Administrator explicitly justifies it.

Requirement 24: The system shall provide configurable justification thresholds and rules (e.g., certain absence types auto-justify or require documentation) configurable by Administrators.

Requirement 25: The system shall group absences by module and by student for viewing and reporting.

Requirement 26: The system shall display a dedicated alert page for Teaching Staff that lists students with more than three unjustified absences and allow filtering by module, period, and cohort.

Requirement 27: The system shall allow Administrators to configure the unjustified-absence alert threshold per module and globally.

Requirement 28: The system shall send configurable notifications (email and optional in-app) to Students, Teachers, Referring Teachers, and Administrative Staff when key events occur (absence recorded, justification submitted, justification approved/rejected, alert threshold exceeded).

Requirement 29: The system shall allow users to opt into or out of notification channels subject to institution policy.

Requirement 30: The system shall maintain a complete audit log of all actions (creation, modification, deletion, approvals) capturing actor, role, timestamp, and before/after values.

Requirement 31: The system shall provide Administrators and Administrative Staff with bulk operations for justifying, rejecting, exporting, and deleting absence records with confirmation and audit logging.

Requirement 32: The system shall allow export of absence reports by module, student, teacher, date range, and justification status to CSV and PDF formats.

Requirement 33: The system shall provide dashboards showing aggregate statistics such as total absences, unjustified absences, absences per module, absences per student, and trend charts over time.

Requirement 34: The system shall allow Teachers and Referring Teachers to see dashboards limited to modules and students they are authorized to view.

Requirement 35: The system shall support pagination and efficient server-side filtering for large absence datasets.

Requirement 36: The system shall allow Administrators to define and manage cohorts, classes, or groups of students and to enroll or remove students from modules in bulk.

Requirement 37: The system shall validate that a Teacher recording an absence is authorized for the specified module and return an error if not authorized.

Requirement 38: The system shall allow retroactive entry and editing of absences with mandatory justification for late entries, and shall log who made the change and why.

Requirement 39: The system shall include a confirmation step and undo option for destructive actions (e.g., deletion of absences or user accounts) and retain deleted records in a recoverable archive for a configurable retention period.

Requirement 40: The system shall support attachment uploads with allowed file type restrictions, file size limits, virus scanning, and encrypted storage.

Requirement 41: The system shall enforce input validation on all forms including required fields, valid date/time formats, file size/type checks, and maximum comment lengths.

Requirement 42: The system shall handle exceptional cases such as overlapping module sessions, duplicate enrollments, and cross-listed modules by allowing Administrators to resolve conflicts.

Requirement 43: The system shall provide a visible status indicator for each absence (e.g., pending justification, justified, rejected, waived).

Requirement 44: The system shall allow Teachers to add provisional notes on an absence that are visible to Administrative Staff and Referring Teachers but not editable by Students.

Requirement 45: The system shall allow Students to view their full absence history with details, justification status, administrative comments, and attached documents.

Requirement 46: The system shall provide role-based access control so Teachers can only view absences for students in their assigned modules and Referring Teachers can view and manage students/modules assigned to them.

Requirement 47: The system shall provide Administrators and Administrative Staff with the ability to create custom absence reason codes and map them to justification policies.

Requirement 48: The system shall provide an API (RESTful or GraphQL) for integration with external Student Information Systems (SIS), timetable systems, and single sign-on (SSO) providers.

Requirement 49: The system shall support single sign-on (SSO) integration (SAML, OAuth2/OpenID Connect) and fall back to local authentication when SSO is unavailable.

Requirement 50: The system shall provide configurable session timeout and secure session management with automatic logout after inactivity.

Requirement 51: The system shall enforce strong password policies (minimum length, complexity, prevention of common passwords) and support password reset via secure tokens.

Requirement 52: The system shall encrypt all data in transit using TLS and encrypt sensitive data at rest according to institutional policy.

Requirement 53: The system shall implement role-based authorization checks on the server side for every sensitive API and page.

Requirement 54: The system shall sanitize and escape all user-provided text to prevent XSS attacks and use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection.

Requirement 55: The system shall implement rate limiting and abuse protections on public endpoints (login, registration, API) to mitigate brute-force and denial-of-service attacks.

Requirement 56: The system shall provide administrator-configurable data retention and automatic anonymization or deletion of records to comply with privacy regulations.

Requirement 57: The system shall maintain backups of the database and uploaded files on a schedule defined by the institution, with documented restore procedures and testing.

Requirement 58: The system shall log security events and provide an alerting mechanism to Administrators for suspicious activity (multiple failed logins, bulk deletions).

Requirement 59: The system shall be resilient to failures and provide high availability with a target uptime SLA (e.g., 99.5%) and documented maintenance windows.

Requirement 60: The system shall provide performance targets such that common pages (dashboards, absence lists, search results) respond within two seconds under normal load.

Requirement 61: The system shall be horizontally scalable to support the institution’s peak concurrent user load with an initial minimum capacity for 500 concurrent users.

Requirement 62: The system shall provide a mobile-responsive user interface that works on common browsers and screen sizes.

Requirement 63: The system shall comply with accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1 AA) so that users with disabilities can navigate and use all core features.

Requirement 64: The system shall support multiple languages/localization for UI text, date/time formats, and notifications.

Requirement 65: The system shall provide contextual help and user documentation accessible in the application for each role (Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, Administrative Staff).

Requirement 66: The system shall provide a searchable activity history for each student showing module attendance, justifications, administrative actions, and exports.

Requirement 67: The system shall implement fine-grained permissions so Administrators can grant or revoke capabilities to other Administrators and staff.

Requirement 68: The system shall provide a reconciliation tool to detect and report inconsistencies between module rosters and recorded absences.

Requirement 69: The system shall allow Administrators and Administrative Staff to schedule recurring module sessions (timetabled classes) and auto-generate session entries for Teachers to mark attendance.

Requirement 70: The system shall allow Teachers to mark attendance for an entire session (present/absent) with the ability to override individual student entries.

Requirement 71: The system shall allow partial attendances (e.g., half-session) to be recorded with a standard notation and associated impact on absence counts.

Requirement 72: The system shall allow Administrators to define and configure business rules for absence counting (e.g., number of lates equals one absence) and apply those rules to alerts and reports.

Requirement 73: The system shall provide a workflow for dispute resolution where Students or Teachers can request review of an absence record and Administrators can record outcomes.

Requirement 74: The system shall provide email templates for institutional notices related to absences that Administrators can customize.

Requirement 75: The system shall provide time zone handling and validation to ensure absence timestamps are accurate across regionally distributed users.

Requirement 76: The system shall handle daylight saving time transitions correctly when recording and displaying session times and absences.

Requirement 77: The system shall provide role-specific dashboards: global metrics for Administrators, module and student metrics for Referring Teachers and Teachers, and personal metrics for Students.

Requirement 78: The system shall provide bulk import and bulk edit capabilities for absence records with validation and error reporting.

Requirement 79: The system shall provide a permissions-based audit report that can be exported to show who accessed or modified sensitive student attendance data.

Requirement 80: The system shall require explicit consent or institutional acknowledgment for storage and processing of student personal data during onboarding.

Requirement 81: The system shall mask or redact personally identifiable information in exports when required by privacy settings or roles.

Requirement 82: The system shall support configurable retention and archival policies for attachments (medical documents) with restricted access and audit trail.

Requirement 83: The system shall provide an administrative interface to configure global and per-module notification preferences and thresholds.

Requirement 84: The system shall perform server-side validation to prevent creation of absences dated in the far future beyond a configurable horizon.

Requirement 85: The system shall provide conflict detection and resolution when multiple Teachers attempt to edit the same absence record concurrently.

Requirement 86: The system shall provide automated daily or weekly summary emails to Referring Teachers and Teachers listing new absences and outstanding justifications.

Requirement 87: The system shall include unit, integration, and end-to-end test coverage for core business logic including authorization, absence counting, and justification workflows.

Requirement 88: The system shall provide a sandbox or staging environment for Administrators to test configuration changes before applying them to production.

Requirement 89: The system shall support a rollback mechanism for configuration changes affecting absence counting rules and alert thresholds.

Requirement 90: The system shall maintain backward compatibility of APIs and data exports for at least two major releases or provide migration tooling.

Requirement 91: The system shall provide configurable retention for deleted records in a soft-delete state and a permanent deletion process with administrator confirmation.

Requirement 92: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to flag absences as sensitive and restrict visibility to a subset of roles when necessary.

Requirement 93: The system shall enable Administrators to configure which roles can create, edit, delete, justify, or export absence records.

Requirement 94: The system shall provide a mechanism to embargo or lock a reporting period so that attendance data for that period cannot be modified without explicit Administrator override.

Requirement 95: The system shall validate and normalize student enrollments against the module roster at the time an absence is recorded and record exceptions when mismatches occur.

Requirement 96: The system shall provide a notification and approval workflow for Referring Teachers to enroll or remove students from modules when bulk changes are made.

Requirement 97: The system shall provide an unobtrusive in-app notification center that stores recent notifications and marks them read/unread.

Requirement 98: The system shall implement pagination and export limits to prevent extremely large single exports without Administrator confirmation.

Requirement 99: The system shall include a dashboard widget or report that highlights students at risk based on configurable rules combining absence counts, trend, and teacher notes.

Requirement 100: The system shall ensure that all actions that affect student records show an explicit confirmation dialog indicating the impact on absence tallies and alerts.

Requirement 101: The system shall provide a configurable retention period for audit logs and a mechanism for secure long-term archival.

Requirement 102: The system shall provide role-specific training materials and an administrative operations manual accessible within the application.

Requirement 103: The system shall implement graceful degradation of non-critical features during partial outages while preserving core attendance recording functionality.

Requirement 104: The system shall support onboarding data synchronization from existing institutional directories and provide conflict-resolution reporting for mismatches.

Requirement 105: The system shall provide fine-grained logging for troubleshooting with options for Administrators to enable verbose logs for short diagnostic windows.

Requirement 106: The system shall support legal and regulatory reporting needs by allowing Administrators to generate time-bounded reports of absences, justifications, and administrative decisions.

Requirement 107: The system shall provide an interface to configure business hours and holiday calendars so that absence recording and notifications respect institutional schedules.

Requirement 108: The system shall permit Administrators to delegate limited administrative privileges to Administrative Staff with scoped access to specific modules or student groups.

Requirement 109: The system shall provide built-in data validation and warnings when importing or synchronizing rosters to avoid duplicate or inconsistent enrollments.

Requirement 110: The system shall store all timestamps in UTC internally and present localized timestamps to users based on their profile settings.

Requirement 111: The system shall allow exporting anonymized datasets for research or analytics while preserving individual privacy.

Requirement 112: The system shall maintain consistent numbering or identifiers for modules, sessions, and absence events for traceability across systems.

Requirement 113: The system shall support an escalation workflow for chronic absenteeism where automatic referrals or alerts are generated after configurable thresholds and approvals.

Requirement 114: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to bulk-apply a single justification reason to multiple absences (e.g., campus-wide event).

Requirement 115: The system shall include automated validation that prevents marking a student as present and absent for the same session.

Requirement 116: The system shall notify Referring Teachers when a student in their care exceeds an absence threshold even if the student’s module teachers have not yet acted.

Requirement 117: The system shall provide a printable view of module attendance rosters for Teachers and Referring Teachers.

Requirement 118: The system shall provide translation-ready UI strings and a mechanism for Administrators to supply institution-specific terminology (e.g., "module" vs "course").

Requirement 119: The system shall provide an administrative audit that lists current configuration that affects absence counting and alerting for transparency and compliance.

Requirement 120: The system shall support scheduled automated tasks (cron-style) for sending reminders, generating reports, and archiving old records with logs of successful/failed runs.

Requirement 1: The system shall provide secure authentication for all users (Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, Administrative Staff) using username and password.

Requirement 2: The system shall support role-based access control (RBAC) enforcing distinct permissions for Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, and Administrative Staff.

Requirement 3: The system shall allow Administrators to create new user accounts with role assignment and mandatory profile fields (name, email, unique ID, role).

Requirement 4: The system shall allow Administrators to edit all user profile fields and role assignments.

Requirement 5: The system shall allow Administrators to deactivate, reactivate, and delete user accounts, with an option for soft-delete (recoverable) and hard-delete (permanent) and configurable retention period before hard-delete.

Requirement 6: The system shall prevent deletion of users who have dependent attendance records unless the Administrator confirms deletion or reassigns/archives those records.

Requirement 7: The system shall maintain an audit log of all user account creations, modifications, deactivations, and deletions recording who, when, and what changed.

Requirement 8: The system shall allow Administrators to create teaching modules with a unique module identifier, name, description, academic period, and associated metadata (credits, schedule template).

Requirement 9: The system shall allow Administrators to edit module data and maintain a version/history log of changes.

Requirement 10: The system shall allow Administrators to delete modules with a configurable policy (soft-delete, cascade archive, or prevent deletion if attendance exists) and require Administrator confirmation before destructive actions.

Requirement 11: The system shall allow Administrators to associate each module with one or more Referring Teachers.

Requirement 12: The system shall permit Administrators to assign or reassign Referring Teachers to modules individually or in bulk.

Requirement 13: The system shall validate that Referring Teachers assigned to a module exist in the global user database.

Requirement 14: The system shall enable Administrators to create, maintain, and delete groups (cohorts/classes) and assign students and teachers to groups.

Requirement 15: The system shall maintain a central, global database of teachers and students managed by Administrators.

Requirement 16: The system shall support import of user, group, and module data in common formats (CSV, XLSX) with duplicate detection and import validation reports.

Requirement 17: The system shall provide export of user, group, module, and attendance data in CSV and PDF formats with configurable columns.

Requirement 18: The system shall allow Referring Teachers to add Teachers and Students to modules or groups only if those users already exist in the global database.

Requirement 19: The system shall present Referring Teachers with a searchable list of existing users to link to modules or groups.

Requirement 20: The system shall prevent Referring Teachers from creating new global user accounts; only Administrators may create new users.

Requirement 21: The system shall allow Teachers to mark attendance for a given module session and for individual students (present, absent, late, excused, partial) and to record the date and time of the session.

Requirement 22: The system shall allow Teachers to report an absence for any student enrolled in one of their modules.

Requirement 23: The system shall allow Teachers to add a free-text comment when they record an absence for a student.

Requirement 24: The system shall store each attendance record with: student ID, module ID, teacher ID (who recorded), session date/time, status, comment, and timestamp of creation and last modification.

Requirement 25: The system shall allow Teachers to edit or delete attendance records they created within a configurable time window and shall record edits in the audit log.

Requirement 26: The system shall allow Administrators and Administrative Staff to edit attendance records and to add/modify the official reason for absence.

Requirement 27: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to enter the official reason for an absence and mark it as justified or unjustified.

Requirement 28: The system shall support a configurable set of absence reasons (illness, medical appointment, family emergency, school activity, other) and allow free-text custom reasons.

Requirement 29: The system shall allow Students to view their own attendance records, comments, and justification status.

Requirement 30: The system shall allow Students to submit an absence justification request with free-text explanation and attach supporting documents (images, PDFs) with configurable size and file-type limits.

Requirement 31: The system shall notify Administrative Staff and the Referring Teacher when a Student submits a justification request for an absence.

Requirement 32: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to upload and attach supporting documents to attendance records.

Requirement 33: The system shall maintain an immutable history of status changes for each absence (reported -> justification submitted -> reason entered -> justified/unjustified) with timestamps and actor IDs.

Requirement 34: The system shall allow Teachers and Referring Teachers to view the list of absences for all students enrolled in any of their modules.

Requirement 35: The system shall allow Referring Teachers to view module-level and student-level attendance reports for modules they are assigned to.

Requirement 36: The system shall allow Administrative Staff and Administrators to view all student absences across the institution.

Requirement 37: The system shall present absences grouped by module and by student with interactive filters (date range, status, reason, teacher, group).

Requirement 38: The system shall offer aggregation views (counts per student, counts per module, counts per date range) and support sorting by count, name, and date.

Requirement 39: The system shall provide a configurable alert page that lists students with more than three unjustified absences and shall allow filtering by module, group, and date range.

Requirement 40: The system shall make the alert page visible to Teaching Staff (Teachers and Referring Teachers) and to Administrative Staff and Administrators.

Requirement 41: The system shall treat "unjustified absence" as an absence record where Administrative Staff has not marked the absence as justified and/or has marked it explicitly as unjustified, subject to configurable business rules.

Requirement 42: The system shall provide a configurable threshold for the number of unjustified absences that trigger alerts and define whether the threshold is cumulative or within a configurable period (e.g., per semester, last 30 days).

Requirement 43: The system shall send automated notifications (email and optional SMS) to relevant Teachers, Referring Teachers, Administrative Staff, and optionally Students when a student exceeds the unjustified absence threshold.

Requirement 44: The system shall allow Teachers and Referring Teachers to acknowledge alerts and add notes or remediation plans to the student's attendance record.

Requirement 45: The system shall allow bulk marking of attendance for a class session (mark all present, then mark absentees) and save change sets atomically.

Requirement 46: The system shall support partial attendance statuses (late arrival with minutes late, early leave with minutes, present for only part) and reflect them in aggregated absence/attendance counts according to configurable rules.

Requirement 47: The system shall prevent Teachers from recording attendance for modules and sessions they are not authorized for.

Requirement 48: The system shall allow scheduling of module sessions (date/time, location) and associate attendance records with a scheduled session or ad-hoc session.

Requirement 49: The system shall expose a calendar or session list to Teachers and Referring Teachers showing upcoming sessions they are responsible for.

Requirement 50: The system shall allow Teachers to retrospectively record attendance for past sessions within a configurable limit and flag retrospective entries as such.

Requirement 51: The system shall support conflict detection (e.g., two sessions same time for the same room or teacher) and warn Administrators when creating schedules.

Requirement 52: The system shall provide a search function for users, modules, groups, and attendance records with filters and saved queries.

Requirement 53: The system shall provide printable and downloadable attendance reports per student, per module, per group, and per period, with configurable formats.

Requirement 54: The system shall provide role-appropriate dashboards summarizing key metrics: for Teachers — recent absences and alerts for their modules; for Referring Teachers — module summaries and enrollment actions; for Administrative Staff — institution-wide absence statistics; for Students — personal attendance summary.

Requirement 55: The system shall provide an API (REST or GraphQL) for integration with third-party systems (student information systems, timetable systems) with token-based authentication and role-based access.

Requirement 56: The system shall provide import/update operations for enrollment lists to allow Administrators to sync students and teachers with external systems.

Requirement 57: The system shall validate all inputs and prevent invalid data entry (missing required fields, invalid date ranges, duplicate enrollments).

Requirement 58: The system shall enforce uniqueness constraints for user email and unique ID and for module identifiers.

Requirement 59: The system shall display clear, contextual error messages for validation failures to end users.

Requirement 60: The system shall send email notifications for critical actions: account created, password reset, attendance recorded (optional per user preferences), justification status changed, and alert thresholds exceeded.

Requirement 61: The system shall allow users to set notification preferences (email on/off, daily digest, push notifications if supported).

Requirement 62: The system shall implement session management with automatic logout after configurable inactivity timeout and prevent concurrent sessions if configured.

Requirement 63: The system shall provide secure password-reset mechanisms with multi-step verification (email token) and enforce password strength policies.

Requirement 64: The system shall support optional two-factor authentication for privileged roles (Administrators and Administrative Staff).

Requirement 65: The system shall provide a user-friendly interface that is responsive and usable on desktop and mobile browsers.

Requirement 66: The system shall conform to accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1 AA) to support users with disabilities.

Requirement 67: The system shall offer UI localization and language selection for all visible text with default language set and ability to add translations.

Requirement 68: The system shall implement role-based visibility so students see only their own data and teachers see only modules/groups they are assigned to unless given expanded permissions.

Requirement 69: The system shall implement fine-grained permission controls for Administrators to grant temporary or limited elevated privileges (e.g., escalate a Teacher to Referring Teacher for a module).

Requirement 70: The system shall provide an administrative interface for configuring business rules: absence reason codes, justification workflow, unjustified absence threshold, retrospective recording window, file upload policies, notification templates.

Requirement 71: The system shall provide activity/audit logs for all attendance record actions (create, update, delete, justify) including actor, timestamps, previous values, and IP address.

Requirement 72: The system shall allow Administrators to search and filter audit logs and export them for compliance.

Requirement 73: The system shall store uploaded documents securely and link them to the related attendance record, with configurable retention and deletion policies.

Requirement 74: The system shall implement file scanning for malware on uploaded attachments and block unsafe file types.

Requirement 75: The system shall provide data retention and archival policies configurable by Administrator to archive old attendance records and associated documents.

Requirement 76: The system shall encrypt sensitive data in transit (TLS) and store sensitive fields (passwords, personal identifiers) encrypted at rest.

Requirement 77: The system shall comply with applicable privacy regulations (e.g., GDPR) including consent records, data subject access requests, and the ability to export or erase a student's personal data in accordance with policy and retention rules.

Requirement 78: The system shall implement role-appropriate data masking in exports and on-screen for non-authorized users.

Requirement 79: The system shall enforce rate limiting and input throttling to protect against automated abuse.

Requirement 80: The system shall be designed for high availability with defined uptime SLA (configurable by deployment) and graceful degradation of non-critical features during partial outages.

Requirement 81: The system shall respond to UI requests within acceptable performance thresholds (e.g., pages load within 2 seconds under normal load) and provide pagination for large result sets.

Requirement 82: The system shall scale horizontally to support growing numbers of users and concurrent attendance operations.

Requirement 83: The system shall provide scheduled automated backups of the database and file storage with configurable retention and tested disaster recovery procedures.

Requirement 84: The system shall support transactional integrity for bulk attendance operations so that incomplete operations are rolled back on error.

Requirement 85: The system shall prevent race conditions when multiple users edit the same attendance record, using optimistic locking or edit locks, and present merge/conflict resolution prompts.

Requirement 86: The system shall provide comprehensive automated unit and integration tests for core attendance and user-management functionality, and support deployment pipelines.

Requirement 87: The system shall provide data validation for date/time entries with timezone awareness and clear handling of daylight saving transitions.

Requirement 88: The system shall guard against fraudulent justification submissions by logging submitter IP and device and allowing Administrative Staff to request additional verification.

Requirement 89: The system shall provide an interface for Administrative Staff to bulk-justify or bulk-unjustify absences with audit recording.

Requirement 90: The system shall allow Administrators to reassign Referring Teachers or Teachers for a module and automatically notify affected users.

Requirement 91: The system shall allow Administrators to merge duplicate user accounts and merge associated attendance records with conflict resolution and audit trail.

Requirement 92: The system shall handle orphaned attendance records (e.g., user deleted) by offering options to reassign, anonymize, or archive while preserving compliance.

Requirement 93: The system shall provide role-based printable notifications or letters (e.g., warning letters for excessive unjustified absences) with placeholders for student and attendance data.

Requirement 94: The system shall provide a configurable retention period and archival process for alert states and resolved alerts.

Requirement 95: The system shall allow Teachers to filter attendance lists by status (present/absent/justified/unjustified/late) and to search within lists.

Requirement 96: The system shall provide inline help, tooltips, and a user manual accessible from the UI for all roles.

Requirement 97: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to import external justifications (doctor’s notes) and attach them to corresponding absence records.

Requirement 98: The system shall implement secure API keys and OAuth2 token mechanisms for integrations with external systems and revoke tokens on demand.

Requirement 99: The system shall provide notifications and logs for integration failures and data synchronization errors with retry and alert mechanisms.

Requirement 100: The system shall allow configurable business hours and contact details for modules and staff to be displayed on relevant pages.

Requirement 101: The system shall provide a sandbox/test environment for Administrators and developers to validate changes without affecting production data.

Requirement 102: The system shall support browser compatibility across modern browsers and degrade gracefully for unsupported browsers.

Requirement 103: The system shall record IP addresses and device/user-agent strings for security-relevant events (login, password reset, attendance modification).

Requirement 104: The system shall allow filtering of alerts and reports by academic period, semester, or custom date ranges.

Requirement 105: The system shall allow Teachers and Referring Teachers to export attendance lists for a session in printable format for in-class use.

Requirement 106: The system shall allow Administrators to configure what roles receive which notifications and the cadence (real-time, daily digest).

Requirement 107: The system shall provide a configurable retention / purge schedule for uploaded files and logs with Administrator override.

Requirement 108: The system shall provide clear on-screen confirmation and undo capability for destructive actions within a short time frame.

Requirement 109: The system shall support multi-tenancy or logical partitioning if required for departmental separation, with strict data isolation.

Requirement 110: The system shall include mechanisms for reporting bugs and requesting features from within the UI and track those reports for Administrators.

Requirement 111: The system shall provide analytics dashboards for Administrators showing trends (absence rates over time, modules with highest absence, student risk profiles).

Requirement 112: The system shall allow configuration of which absence statuses count toward unjustified absence thresholds (e.g., late vs absent).
