Requirement 113: The system shall provide role-specific training material and onboarding flows for first-time users.

Requirement 114: The system shall ensure all user-facing text (including error messages) is modifiable by Administrators and translatable.

Requirement 115: The system shall implement secure deployment practices and regular security patching guidance for the hosting environment.

Requirement 116: The system shall provide an emergency contact and override mechanism for Administrators to perform immediate global actions (e.g., freeze records, disable logins) with audit trail.

Requirement 1: The system shall provide role-based authentication for Administrator, Referring Teacher, Teacher, Student, and Administrative Staff accounts.

Requirement 2: The system shall allow users to register accounts subject to administrator approval or pre-registration by the administrator.

Requirement 3: The system shall enforce unique usernames and unique institutional email addresses for all user accounts.

Requirement 4: The system shall require secure passwords that meet configurable complexity rules and shall support password reset via validated email.

Requirement 5: The system shall support multi-factor authentication for administrator and administrative staff accounts.

Requirement 6: The system shall store all user roles and permissions in an auditable, role-based access control (RBAC) model.

Requirement 7: The Administrator shall be able to create, read, update, and delete ("CRUD") teaching modules.

Requirement 8: The Administrator shall be able to assign one or more Referring Teachers to each module.

Requirement 9: The Administrator shall be able to associate existing Teachers and Students to modules or remove those associations.

Requirement 10: The system shall enforce that a Referring Teacher or Teacher may only be assigned to modules if their user account exists in the global database.

Requirement 11: The Administrator shall be able to create, read, update, and delete user accounts for all roles.

Requirement 12: The Administrator shall be able to define and manage module metadata including module code, title, description, scheduled sessions, academic term, and capacity.

Requirement 13: The Administrator shall be able to configure module schedules (dates, start/end times, recurring patterns) and associate sessions with modules.

Requirement 14: The system shall maintain referential integrity between modules, users, enrollments, and absence records.

Requirement 15: The global database of Teachers and Students shall be centrally managed by the Administrator and allow search, filtering, and bulk operations.

Requirement 16: The Referring Teacher shall be able to add existing Teachers to a module if those Teachers already exist in the global database.

Requirement 17: The Referring Teacher shall be able to add existing Students to a module if those Students already exist in the global database.

Requirement 18: The Referring Teacher shall be prevented from adding Teachers or Students who are not present in the global database and shall be offered a workflow to request administrator registration.

Requirement 19: The system shall record module enrollments with enrollment date, enrollment source (Admin/Referring Teacher/import), and enrollment status (active/inactive/withdrawn).

Requirement 20: A Teacher shall be able to report an absence for themselves for a specific module session and specify the date and time of the absence.

Requirement 21: When reporting an absence, a Teacher shall be able to add a free-text comment limited to a configurable maximum length.

Requirement 22: The system shall record each absence with a unique identifier, reporting user, reporting role, timestamp, associated module and session (if any), and original comment.

Requirement 23: A Teacher shall be able to view the list of absences for all Students enrolled in any module in which that Teacher is assigned.

Requirement 24: A Teacher shall be able to filter and sort student absence lists by module, student, date range, justification status, reason, and severity.

Requirement 25: Administrative Staff shall have access to view, search, filter, and export all student absences across all modules.

Requirement 26: Administrative Staff shall be able to enter or update the official reason for any absence and mark absences as justified, unjustified, or pending.

Requirement 27: Students shall be able to view their own absence records including date, module, reporting teacher, comments, official reason, and justification status.

Requirement 28: Students shall be able to submit justifications for their absences that include free-text explanation and optional supporting document uploads (PDF, JPG, PNG).

Requirement 29: Administrative Staff shall be able to review student-submitted justifications, attach decisions, and mark absences as justified or rejected with comments.

Requirement 30: The system shall support attachment storage for supporting documents and enforce file type and file size limits configurable by the Administrator.

Requirement 31: The system shall notify relevant parties by configurable channels (email, in-app notification, SMS if enabled) when an absence is reported, when a student submits a justification, and when a justification is accepted or rejected.

Requirement 32: The system shall maintain an auditable history (who, what, when) for each absence record and for all changes to status, reasons, comments, and attachments.

Requirement 33: Absences shall be groupable and viewable aggregated by module and by student.

Requirement 34: The system shall provide summary dashboards showing counts of absences by module, by student, by date range, and by justification status.

Requirement 35: The system shall provide a dedicated page listing students who have more than three unjustified absences and allow filtering by module, term, and date range.

Requirement 36: The threshold for the alert page ("more than three unjustified absences") shall be configurable by the Administrator and may be applied per term, per rolling period, or per module.

Requirement 37: The system shall automatically compute counts of unjustified absences per student and update alerts in real time upon status changes.

Requirement 38: The system shall notify the assigned Teachers and Referring Teachers when a student exceeds the configured unjustified absence threshold.

Requirement 39: The system shall allow Teachers and Referring Teachers to acknowledge or dismiss alerts with an auditable reason.

Requirement 40: The system shall provide role-restricted dashboards: Administrators with system-wide controls, Administrative Staff with full absence handling privileges, Referring Teachers with module-level enrolment controls, Teachers with reporting and viewing privileges, and Students with personal records access.

Requirement 41: The system shall provide search functionality across users, modules, and absences with support for partial matches and multiple filters.

Requirement 42: The system shall support pagination and efficient querying for large datasets and expose export functionality (CSV, XLSX, PDF) for reports.

Requirement 43: The system shall provide an API for integration with external Student Information Systems (SIS) for user and enrollment synchronization, with authentication and authorization.

Requirement 44: The system shall support bulk import and export of users, module assignments, and historical absences via CSV with validation reports for errors.

Requirement 45: The system shall prevent duplicate absence reports for the same user, same module session, and same timestamp and shall warn users of potential duplicates with an override only for authorized roles.

Requirement 46: The system shall allow editing and deletion of absence records only by authorized roles and shall record edits/deletions in the audit trail.

Requirement 47: When a module or user is deleted, the system shall provide options to cascade delete, anonymize, or retain associated absence records, and the chosen behavior shall be confirmed and auditable.

Requirement 48: The system shall provide configurable retention and archival policies for absence records and attachments in compliance with institutional rules.

Requirement 49: The system shall validate all inputs including dates, file uploads, numeric fields, and enforce referential constraints on module and user references.

Requirement 50: The system shall support concurrent access and optimistic locking to prevent lost updates when multiple users modify the same absence or enrollment records simultaneously.

Requirement 51: The system shall expose role-based activity logs showing sign-in events, actions performed, and IP addresses for administrative review.

Requirement 52: The system shall enforce session timeouts and automatic logout after a configurable period of inactivity.

Requirement 53: The system shall provide localized UI strings and support for at least one configurable language with easy addition of more languages.

Requirement 54: The system shall be responsive and usable on desktop, tablet, and mobile web browsers.

Requirement 55: The system shall meet WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility standards for screen-reader compatibility, keyboard navigation, and color contrast.

Requirement 56: The system shall encrypt sensitive personal data at rest and in transit using industry-standard algorithms (e.g., TLS for transit and AES-256 for storage).

Requirement 57: The system shall enforce least-privilege access controls to sensitive fields such as medical justification notes.

Requirement 58: The system shall provide role-based data visibility masks for confidential information when required by policy.

Requirement 59: The system shall maintain backups of the database and attachments at a configurable cadence and allow restoration to a point-in-time within the backup retention window.

Requirement 60: The system shall provide audit logs that cannot be tampered with by non-administrative users and shall be retained according to retention policies.

Requirement 61: The system shall perform input validation and return user-friendly error messages for invalid or missing required fields.

Requirement 62: The system shall provide configurable email templates for notifications and allow previews before sending.

Requirement 63: The system shall throttle or rate-limit automated notifications to prevent spamming recipients.

Requirement 64: The system shall provide configurable business rules for how justifications are processed (e.g., auto-approve certain document types, require manual approval).

Requirement 65: The system shall support configurable absence types such as excused, unexcused, tardy/late, and support mapping rules by Administrative Staff.

Requirement 66: The system shall allow Teachers to view historical attendance trends and charts for individual students and modules.

Requirement 67: The system shall permit Administrative Staff to generate and download periodic reports (daily/weekly/monthly/termly) on absences segmented by module, student, teacher, and justification status.

Requirement 68: The system shall provide exportable audit-ready reports with timestamps and user identifiers for official record keeping.

Requirement 69: The system shall provide fine-grained permission controls for which roles can mark absence justification, edit reasons, delete records, and export data.

Requirement 70: The system shall support configurable notification recipient lists per module (e.g., module teachers, referring teachers, administrative staff).

Requirement 71: The system shall allow students to contest or appeal justification rejections through an internal workflow routed to Administrative Staff and Referring Teacher as configured.

Requirement 72: The system shall support escalation rules that notify higher-level administrators if a student's unjustified absence count exceeds escalating thresholds.

Requirement 73: The system shall prevent teachers from falsely reporting absences via review, requiring confirmation for reporting for others and logging originator IP and device.

Requirement 74: The system shall provide the ability for Referring Teachers to request administrator action to register new users not found in the database and track those requests.

Requirement 75: The system shall track academic term and support queries scoped to a specific academic term or date range.

Requirement 76: The system shall validate that reported absences reference a valid scheduled session for the module when session data exists, and allow free-date reporting otherwise.

Requirement 77: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to set default absence reasons and maintain a taxonomy of standardized reasons.

Requirement 78: The system shall allow custom free-text reasons and map them to standardized categories where possible.

Requirement 79: The system shall provide confirmation dialogs for destructive operations (delete, irreversible changes) and require explicit confirmation by authorized users.

Requirement 80: The system shall provide user activity notifications in an in-application inbox as well as optional email/SMS.

Requirement 81: The system shall provide a configurable dashboard widget for Teachers and Referring Teachers showing recent absences, pending justifications, and alerts.

Requirement 82: The system shall ensure sub-second response times for common queries under normal load and define acceptable SLAs (e.g., 95th percentile page load <2s for dashboards).

Requirement 83: The system shall scale to support the institution's maximum expected number of concurrent users with horizontal scaling options for application and database tiers.

Requirement 84: The system shall maintain high availability with planned maintenance windows announced in advance and a target uptime of 99.5% per term.

Requirement 85: The system shall implement secure coding practices and regular vulnerability scanning and address critical vulnerabilities within a defined SLA.

Requirement 86: The system shall log and monitor errors and performance metrics and provide alerting to system administrators for outage conditions.

Requirement 87: The system shall provide clear help text, inline field validation, and a searchable help/FAQ section for users.

Requirement 88: The system shall provide a role-based administrative UI to configure business rules, notification thresholds, file size limits, retention periods, and absence types.

Requirement 89: The system shall support data export in machine-readable formats and provide an audit of exports and which user performed them.

Requirement 90: The system shall enforce privacy controls to comply with applicable data protection regulations (e.g., GDPR), including data subject access requests, the right to be forgotten, and export of personal data.

Requirement 91: The system shall provide retainment and deletion workflows that ensure legal hold exceptions can be applied to specific records.

Requirement 92: The system shall include unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for core workflows including absence reporting, justification, approval, and alert generation.

Requirement 93: The system shall provide an administrative audit trail viewer that supports filtering by date range, user, action type, and affected records.

Requirement 94: The system shall implement transactional integrity so that multi-step operations (e.g., bulk imports, enrollment changes) are atomic and can be rolled back in case of errors.

Requirement 95: The system shall protect against common web threats including CSRF, XSS, SQL injection, and implement CSP and security headers.

Requirement 96: The system shall allow configuration of business hours and holiday calendars so absence reporting and alerts can account for non-instructional days.

Requirement 97: The system shall support timezone-aware date/time handling and display times according to user preferences or institution default timezone.

Requirement 98: The system shall provide an impersonation feature for Administrators to securely assume another user's view for troubleshooting with explicit logging and approval.

Requirement 99: The system shall provide a configurable escalation and notification retry policy for failed message deliveries.

Requirement 100: The system shall provide mechanisms to reconcile and merge duplicate user accounts while preserving associated absence history and audit trails.

Requirement 101: The system shall validate and enforce module capacity constraints if an institution requires enrollment limits.

Requirement 102: The system shall allow teachers to mark a student as present, absent, tardy, or excused for specific sessions and optionally record duration of absence or lateness.

Requirement 103: The system shall provide a bulk absence reporting UI for Teachers and Administrative Staff for cases like field trips or large-scale events.

Requirement 104: The system shall provide a mechanism for Teachers to attach session-level notes (e.g., class canceled) that affect absence interpretation.

Requirement 105: The system shall display computed statistics such as percent attendance per module and per student for configured periods.

Requirement 106: The system shall support scheduled automated reports to be emailed to selected recipients on configurable schedules.

Requirement 107: The system shall provide role-appropriate dashboards and restrict access to sensitive aggregation data to authorized roles.

Requirement 108: The system shall implement graceful error handling with clear recovery steps and error identifiers for support.

Requirement 109: The system shall provide a secure admin operations dashboard to track system health, backups, and recent exports.

Requirement 110: The system shall support versioning of module definitions so historical sessions and absences consistently reference the module state at the time of the event.

Requirement 111: The system shall support configurable retention and archival storage tiers to optimize cost for large attachment volumes.

Requirement 112: The system shall require explicit consent for communications and store user communication preferences.

Requirement 113: The system shall provide integration points for single sign-on (SSO) via SAML or OAuth2 for institutional identity providers.

Requirement 114: The system shall include rate limits and abuse detection for public-facing endpoints to protect against denial-of-service attempts.

Requirement 115: The system shall provide data validation and human-readable import error reporting for bulk uploads, with the ability to correct and re-run failed records.

Requirement 116: The system shall provide a consented audit and reporting mechanism when Administrative Staff modifies sensitive justification data.

Requirement 117: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to attach internal-only notes to absence records visible only to staff and administrators.

Requirement 118: The system shall allow configurable retention of anonymized or aggregated data for analytical purposes while protecting personally identifiable information.

Requirement 119: The system shall provide a dispute-resolution workflow preserving all communications and actions taken with timestamps and actors.

Requirement 120: The system shall include documentation for administrators, teachers, and students describing workflows, permissions, and contact points for help.

Requirement 121: The system shall provide a sandbox or staging environment for testing configuration changes without affecting production data.

Requirement 122: The system shall enforce sensible defaults on all configurable settings and provide role-based safeguards to prevent misconfiguration that could lead to data loss.

Requirement 123: The system shall support periodic data synchronization tasks and provide monitoring and reporting on synchronization success/failure.

Requirement 124: The system shall allow administrators to configure whether teachers may directly justify student absences or only submit comments and leave justification to Administrative Staff.

Requirement 125: The system shall allow Administrative Staff to tag absences with follow-up actions (e.g., parental contact required) and track completion of those actions.

Requirement 126: The system shall provide secure deletion workflows that permanently remove files from storage within the constraints of retention and legal hold rules.

Requirement 127: The system shall enforce provenance on every absence or justification action by recording the source device, IP, and user agent where feasible.

Requirement 128: The system shall provide exportable compliance reports summarizing retention actions, deletions, and legal holds.
